sections.paragraph:
καταβαρύνω pass. aor. subj. 1 pl. καταβαρυνθῶμεν 2 Km 13:25 (s. prec. entry; Theophr. et al.; LXX) weigh down, burden, oppress τὴν ζωήν make someone’s life hard (cp. τὸν βίον Antip. in Stob. 4, 22, 25 vol. IV 511, 7 H.) Hm 12, 6, 2. Pass. (Herm. Wr. 2, 9) of the eyes ἦσαν καταβαρυνόμενοι were heavy=fell shut Mk 14:40.—DELG s.v. βαρύς.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

aor. aor. = aorist

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

  1. pl. = plural

prec. prec. = preceding

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Hm Hm = Mandates

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 514
